metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the plan's claim that the live Jira test is 'read-only / uniquely-tagged': it creates and deletes real Jira issues in project 'DIG' (or JIRA_PROJECT env); specify the actual isolation strategy (e.g., a dedicated `JIRA_PROJECT` secret pointing to a sandboxed test project) so the scheduled canary's mutation scope is bounded.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the live Jira test 'targets a read-only / uniquely-tagged project (no mass mutation)', but reading test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py shows it calls client.create_issue() twice and client.delete_issue() in finally — it MUTATES the Jira project. There is no unique-tag enforcement; the project key defaults to 'DIG' with no isolation guard. The 'non-destructive' claim rests on cleanup running, not on read-only access.
findings.location: Plan section 'What', item 1: 'Keep it non-destructive: the live test targets a read-only / uniquely-tagged project (no mass mutation).'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'targets a read-only / uniquely-tagged project (no mass mutation)' with: 'creates and deletes probe issues in a dedicated sandboxed Jira project (passed via a `JIRA_PROJECT` repository secret/variable); the workflow should map `JIRA_PROJECT` alongside the other Jira secrets so the scheduled run is isolated from any production project.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an `acli` binary installation step to the `external-integration.yml` external job (mirroring the cache + download + verify + PATH-add block in `reconcile-bridge.yml`) so that `shutil.which('acli')` resolves on the runner and `_live_jira_ready()` can return `True` when JIRA_* secrets are mapped.
findings.finding: The plan maps JIRA_* credentials into external-integration.yml but does not install the `acli` binary, which all live Jira tests require (via `shutil.which('acli') is not None` in every `_live_jira_ready()` gate). On the scheduled run, even with secrets correctly configured, every Jira test will skip with 'no live Jira creds / acli binary' — triggering the all-skip canary as a false positive and making the canary permanently RED on a correctly-wired setup.
findings.location: What §1 / external-integration.yml schedule job / Acceptance Criteria AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Copy the 'Cache acli binary', 'Download + verify + install acli', and 'Add acli to PATH' steps from `.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml` (lines 136-185) into the `external` job in `external-integration.yml`, referencing the same `vars.ACLI_VERSION` / `vars.ACLI_SHA256` / `secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN` / `vars.JIRA_URL` / `vars.JIRA_USER` variables. The acli auth login step should follow the credential-mapping env block so the binary is both present and authenticated before pytest runs.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify the plan covers installing the `acli` binary on the scheduled runner (e.g., add an install step to the workflow), or document that `shutil.which('acli')` is an additional prerequisite alongside the three JIRA_* secrets — otherwise the canary fires red even after secrets are correctly configured.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that mapping JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER/JIRA_API_TOKEN into the workflow env is sufficient for the live Jira test to authenticate and execute, but test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py::_live_jira_ready() also hard-gates on `shutil.which('acli')` (the Atlassian CLI binary). On a stock ubuntu-latest runner, `acli` is not pre-installed. With secrets configured but `acli` absent, the test still hard-skips — causing the proposed fail-on-all-skip canary to fire red on every scheduled run, generating persistent false alarms even after operator secret configuration.
findings.location: What § 1 ('Schedule + wire Jira secrets') and AC 'JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER/JIRA_API_TOKEN … so the live Jira test can authenticate'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a workflow step (e.g., `sudo apt-get install -y acli` or a download+install from Atlassian's release endpoint) before the pytest run step in the `external` job. Mirror the pattern of the existing `Ensure jq is installed` step. Then update the AC operator-attested item to include `acli` binary availability as a prerequisite alongside the JIRA_* secrets.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether JIRA_URL and JIRA_USER should be sourced from `vars.*` (as in reconcile-bridge.yml) or `secrets.*`, and align the plan's wiring instruction, the config-as-artifact test assertion, and the operator-attested secret-config AC bullet consistently.
findings.finding: The plan proposes mapping JIRA_URL and JIRA_USER from secrets.* but the existing repo workflow (reconcile-bridge.yml) stores them as vars.* (GitHub Actions repository variables, non-secret), with only JIRA_API_TOKEN as a secret. If the external-integration.yml implementation uses secrets.JIRA_URL and secrets.JIRA_USER while the values are registered as vars, the expressions will silently resolve to empty strings, causing the Jira tests to skip and the canary to remain a false green.
findings.location: What section, item 1 / Acceptance Criteria first bullet: 'maps JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER/JIRA_API_TOKEN from secrets into the external job env'
findings.suggested_fix: Mirror reconcile-bridge.yml: use `vars.JIRA_URL` and `vars.JIRA_USER` (non-sensitive) plus `secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN` (sensitive). Update the plan's wording to 'map JIRA_URL / JIRA_USER from vars.* and JIRA_API_TOKEN from secrets.*', and update the config-as-artifact test to assert the correct expression forms.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an `acli` download/install step to the `external` job in `external-integration.yml` (mirroring the `reconcile-bridge.yml` pattern: download from `https://acli.atlassian.com/linux/${ACLI_VERSION}/...`, verify checksum, add to PATH, and authenticate) so that `_live_jira_ready()` can return True when Jira credentials are present.
findings.finding: The live Jira external tests gate on `shutil.which('acli') is not None` (verified in test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py:67, test_link_sync_live.py:36, test_reconcile_live.py:38, test_verified_fake_contract_live.py:47), but the plan adds only JIRA_* env vars and a cron trigger to external-integration.yml — it does not install the `acli` binary. Without acli on PATH, `_live_jira_ready()` returns False and all four Jira tests skip regardless of credentials, causing the all-skip canary to fire as a false alarm (or, if the canary distinguishes this case, the live Jira path is never exercised). The reconcile-bridge.yml workflow has a full acli download/verify/install step that serves as reference implementation.
findings.location: What / Scope — .github/workflows/external-integration.yml; also tests/external/test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py:67, test_link_sync_live.py:36, test_reconcile_live.py:38, test_verified_fake_contract_live.py:47
findings.suggested_fix: Copy the 'Cache acli binary', 'Download + verify + install acli', and 'Add acli to PATH and authenticate' steps from .github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml (lines ~136–183) into the `external` job in external-integration.yml, using `ACLI_VERSION` as a repo variable and `JIRA_API_TOKEN` for `acli jira auth login`. This ensures shutil.which('acli') resolves on the runner.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the intended source for JIRA_URL and JIRA_USER: align with the established repo convention (`vars.JIRA_URL` / `vars.JIRA_USER` as non-secret repository variables, mirroring reconcile-bridge.yml), and reserve `secrets.*` for JIRA_API_TOKEN only; declare this access classification explicitly in the plan.
findings.finding: The plan maps JIRA_URL and JIRA_USER from secrets.* but the established repo convention (reconcile-bridge.yml) stores them as vars.* (non-secret GitHub Actions repository variables), leaving the intended access classification undeclared and inconsistent. JIRA_URL and JIRA_USER are non-sensitive config values (a URL and a username/email); treating them as secrets over-classifies them, masks them unnecessarily in CI logs, and pollutes the secrets namespace — reducing the meaningful signal that secrets.* provides for genuinely sensitive values like JIRA_API_TOKEN.
findings.location: What → item 1 / Acceptance Criteria → first bullet: 'maps JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER/JIRA_API_TOKEN from secrets'
findings.suggested_fix: In external-integration.yml, map JIRA_URL: ${{ vars.JIRA_URL }} and JIRA_USER: ${{ vars.JIRA_USER }} (matching reconcile-bridge.yml), and only JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}. Update the AC bullet to read 'maps JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER from vars.* and JIRA_API_TOKEN from secrets.*'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact pytest hook primitive and counter-storage location for the executed-vs-skipped accumulator in `tests/external/conftest.py` (e.g., 'implement `pytest_runtest_logreport` accumulating into a session-attribute dict, then assert in `pytest_sessionfinish`').
findings.finding: The fail-on-all-skip hook's counter accumulation mechanism is never specified: the plan says the hook 'REPORTS executed-vs-skipped counts' and 'FAILS if EVERY external test skipped (zero executed)', but does not name which pytest hook primitive accumulates the count (e.g. `pytest_runtest_logreport`, `pytest_runtest_makereport`, or a `pytest_collection_finish` + item-iteration approach), nor where the running counter lives (module-level global, a plugin class, a session attribute). The existing `tests/external/conftest.py` has no such counter. An implementor has no single authoritative placement, risking silent miscounting if the hook fires at a scope that misses autouse-skipped items.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan: 'In `tests/external/conftest.py`, implement a `pytest_runtest_logreport` hook that, for each report with `report.when == "call"`, increments a session-attribute counter `_ext_executed` on passed/failed outcomes and `_ext_skipped` on skipped outcomes; then in `pytest_sessionfinish`, if `REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL` is truthy and `_ext_executed == 0` and `_ext_skipped > 0`, set `session.exitstatus = pytest.ExitCode.TESTS_FAILED` and emit an error message.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the intended cron expression in the plan (e.g. `0 6 * * 1` — Monday 06:00 UTC) so the executor and the config-as-artifact test can pin the exact schedule rather than defaulting arbitrarily.
findings.finding: The cron schedule is described as 'weekly' but no specific cron expression (day of week, hour, timezone) is given, leaving the executor to choose arbitrarily.
findings.location: What section, item 1: 'add an `on.schedule` cron trigger (weekly)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to item 1: 'Use `cron: "0 6 * * 1"` (Monday 06:00 UTC) as the schedule expression; the config-as-artifact test should assert this exact string is present in the workflow YAML.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the fail-on-all-skip canary in `tests/external/conftest.py` should apply to ALL external sessions (including `langfuse-trace`) or only to sessions that target the Jira path; if Langfuse-only runs should be exempt, scope the canary (e.g., check that at least one Jira-credential-requiring test was collected, not just that any external test ran).
findings.finding: The `langfuse-trace` job in `external-integration.yml` is an unenumerated consumer of the fail-on-all-skip canary. That job also sets `REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL=1` and runs `pytest -m external tests/external/test_llm_trace.py`, so the new hook in `tests/external/conftest.py` will activate for it. If the Langfuse test skips (e.g., the `[tracing]` extra is absent or `LANGFUSE_HOST` is unset in a non-CI invocation), the canary will fail the `langfuse-trace` job — a false-negative for a Jira-focused canary.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a spend/concurrency guard to the plan: specify a `concurrency` group to prevent overlapping scheduled runs, and specify how scheduled-run failures are surfaced to the team (e.g. GitHub Actions failure notification, Slack webhook, or email alert).
findings.finding: The plan adds a weekly schedule trigger that fires the external job, which makes real billable LLM calls (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / OPENAI_API_KEY), but specifies no cost guard, spend cap, concurrency limit, or failure-notification mechanism for unattended scheduled runs.
findings.location: What section, item 1: 'add an on.schedule cron trigger (weekly)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `concurrency: group: external-integration / cancel-in-progress: false` block to the workflow (so a new schedule doesn't cancel a running one, but overlapping runs are queued-not-stacked). Document the failure-notification mechanism (e.g. GitHub's built-in email on workflow failure for repo watchers, or an explicit Slack step).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC requiring a manual `workflow_dispatch` live-run evidence artifact (log excerpt or CI run link) showing the canary fires end-to-end: secrets present → ≥1 external test executes → session green; secrets absent / all-skip → hook triggers → session RED; this evidence should be attached to the PR as the operator-attest.
findings.finding: No acceptance criterion exercises the canary's end-to-end live path: the scheduled workflow firing, running the real pytest external session (with Jira secrets), and the conftest hook correctly detecting all-skip and failing the session. AC2 covers the hook logic via a simulated/unit test, but the actual scheduled-run behavior (hook operating inside a live pytest session against real infrastructure) is never proven.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC3 to: '[operator-attested] A `workflow_dispatch` live run is triggered post-secret-configuration; the run log is attached as PR evidence showing (a) JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER/JIRA_API_TOKEN are visible in the job env, (b) the Jira round-trip test executes (not skipped), and (c) the session exits 0. Additionally, a second dispatch run with secrets removed (or env vars blanked) is triggered to confirm the canary exits non-zero when all tests skip.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
